output: Completed batch 1 task T1. Removed the premature selection-clearing calls from the timeline and gallery shot-creation handlers, verified the timeline editor test module passes, and checkpointed the batch result to `.megaplan/plans/enhance-the-video-editor-20260406-0259/execution_batch_1.json`. The gallery test module still fails due to unrelated pre-existing worktree changes in `GenerationsPaneGallery.tsx` that introduce an unmocked router dependency.
deviations: Both target files already had unrelated unstaged edits in the worktree before this batch. I limited the implementation to the requested selection-clearing removals and did not revert or reshape the other in-progress changes.

The gallery test module currently fails before exercising this batch's change because the existing dirty version of `GenerationsPaneGallery.tsx` now calls `useShotNavigation()` without the test mocking it or rendering under a router. I still ran the full test file with this batch's changes applied, per instructions.

task_updates.executor_notes: Removed `clearSelection()` from timeline `handleCreateShotFromSelection` and `handleGenerateVideoFromSelection`, and removed `clearGallerySelection()` from gallery `createShotFromSelection`. Also removed those cleared-selection functions from the corresponding callback dependency arrays. Verification: `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/` passed. `npx vitest run src/features/gallery/components/GenerationsPane/components/GenerationsPaneGallery.test.tsx` failed due to a pre-existing router-hook issue from unrelated worktree edits in the same file, not from this selection-persistence fix.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Selection now persists after shot creation because the only explicit clears in the shot-creation paths were removed, while the existing menu click-away and Escape handlers still close the menus. Since `selectionShotCreationState` continues to derive from the unchanged selected clip IDs, `canCreateShotFromSelection` remains true on subsequent right-clicks until some other existing selection-dismissal path runs.
